def test_knapsack_exact_capacity_fill():
    # Construct instance where optimal solution exactly fills capacity
    # Capacity = 3, items = [(3, 10), (1, 5)]
    # Optimal: take item with weight=3, value=10 (exactly fills capacity)
    # Buggy code uses weight < j, so when j=3 and weight=3, condition fails (3 < 3 is False)
    # Thus it won't consider taking the item that exactly fills the capacity
    # Expected optimal value is 10, but buggy code returns 0
    assert knapsack(3, [(3, 10), (1, 5)]) == 10

from source import knapsack

def test_knapsack_exact_capacity_fill():
    # Capacity=3, items=[(3,10), (1,5)]
    # Optimal: take item with weight=3, value=10 (exactly fills capacity)
    # Buggy code uses 'weight < j' so when j=3 and weight=3, condition fails
    # Thus it excludes the item that exactly fills capacity
    assert knapsack(3, [(3, 10), (1, 5)]) == 10
